b'
Hello, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Intrepid channel checker Sarah Shapiro is here with the week that was in retail, including The White Lotus nutso flood of brand collaborations. (They definitely took the Barbie approach.)

Feb 21, 2025

Lauren Sherman

Hello, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Intrepid channel checker Sarah \xe2\x80\x9cSShapiro@puck.news\xe2\x80\x9d Shapiro is here with the week that was in retail, including The White Lotus \xe2\x80\x99s nutso flood of brand collaborations. (They definitely took the Barbie approach.) Up top, Sarah also provides a snapshot of consumer spending habits right now, and explains why you\xe2\x80\x99re suddenly seeing Shoreditch-based fashion brand Aligne everywhere these days. (It\xe2\x80\x99s not only because Gabby Katz is the publicist.) And I\xe2\x80\x99ve got some deal details from Tapestry\xe2\x80\x99s sale of Stuart Weitzman to Caleres, which is set to close this summer.

By the time you read this, I\xe2\x80\x99ll be on a plane to New York, where I\xe2\x80\x99m interviewing Alex Mill chairman and all-around legend Mickey Drexler at Columbia Business School\xe2\x80\x99s Retail & Luxury Goods Club Conference on Saturday morning. (If you\xe2\x80\x99re there, please don\xe2\x80\x99t hesitate to say hi while waiting to kiss Mickey\xe2\x80\x99s ring.) After this, it\xe2\x80\x99s over to London, Milan, and\xe2\x80\xa6 Paris! How great. Let\xe2\x80\x99s hang.

Mentioned in this issue : Christian Louboutin, Ralph Lauren, The Row, Beyonc\xc3\xa9 , Aligne, Ginny Seymour , Olivia Muniak , Stuart Weitzman , Tapestry, Joanne Crevoiserat , Miu Miu, Petit Bateau, Zara, Gap, Reformation, Lezio Lopes , The White Lotus , Parker Posey , Net-a-Porter, Mytheresa, and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

Three Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Sarah on getting in A-ligne : If you\xe2\x80\x99ve been noticing Aligne all over your Instagram feed lately\xe2\x80\x94the Daphne Blazer , Leo Long Waisted Vest , and Daphne Knit , all with that suddenly ubiquitous collarless silhouette\xe2\x80\x94it\xe2\x80\x99s likely because the East London\xe2\x80\x93based brand has been orchestrating a carefully calculated expansion into the U.S. market, taking cues from the S\xc3\xa9zane playbook. But whereas Paris-based S\xc3\xa9zane built its American presence primarily through direct-to-consumer channels and calculated scarcity, Aligne is diversifying its approach with a robust wholesale strategy in addition to the usual influencer game.

    Aligne has already landed on Shopbop , an Amazon subsidiary, and C.E.O. Ginny Seymour (who got her start at Saks and was a longtime buyer at Holt Renfrew in Toronto) tells me they are finalizing another major retail partnership for Autumn/Winter 2025. She\xe2\x80\x99s also building a cachet through a very specific subset of New York influencers: the beyond-fashion-literate Leandra Medine Cohen and Harling Ross crowd, but also adjacent personalities, like creative director and self-taught chef Olivia Muniak . During New York Fashion Week, Lauren noted that she attended an Aligne event at Bridges, a hip-because-it\xe2\x80\x99s-strange, very food-people-insider restaurant at Chatham Square, attended by the likes of Arielle Charnas , who, we promise, does not go to a random fashion dinner unless it\xe2\x80\x99s good for business. \xe2\x80\x94Sarah Shapiro

    Aligne is obviously doing business with ShopMy: This is exactly the right mix of decent price and fashion-forward-enough styles to drive major conversions on Substack. It\xe2\x80\x99s a savvy way to enter the American market: Combine old-school retail strategy with influencer targeting, all while making sure your product hits the right price-to-aspiration ratio.
  • And now, Sarah on the January shopping reset : The holiday shopping frenzy has officially wound down, with January 2025 retail sales reflecting the typical post-December dip, according to the Census Bureau report published late last week. The slowdown hit most sectors, with bright spots found mainly in food service, general merchandise stores, and miscellaneous retailers, which in turn suggests that Americans have been prioritizing essentials while cutting back elsewhere. The silver lining? Clothing and accessories stores have been performing better during this window compared to last year, actually, showing that fashion spending remains relatively healthy despite the post-holiday adjustment\xe2\x80\x94perhaps with higher prices offsetting a slightly lower volume of items purchased.

    Meanwhile, Presidents\xe2\x80\x99 and Valentine\xe2\x80\x99s Day sales may boost February numbers, especially via jewelry sales and stores that offered significant promotions. (And remember all the hearts? The trend cycle was in February\xe2\x80\x99s favor.) If spring fever hits early\xe2\x80\x94and the evidence from major markets is that next week is looking much warmer\xe2\x80\x94spending patterns could shift toward seasonal items. We\xe2\x80\x99ll find out when the next retail report lands mid-March. \xe2\x80\x94Sarah Shapiro
  • Lauren on how much Stuart Weitzman is *really* worth : As I noted earlier this week, Tapestry is selling underperforming Stuart Weitzman, which it acquired for more than $500 million a decade ago, to Caleres in a roughly $105 million cash deal. Now I\xe2\x80\x99m told that Caleres\xe2\x80\x94the owner of Dr. Scholl\xe2\x80\x99s, among other shoe things\xe2\x80\x94will also absorb $80 million worth of product from a brand that lost more than $20 million last year. Pretty tough!

    It\xe2\x80\x99s a bit of a drag that Tapestry couldn\xe2\x80\x99t make Stuart Weitzman work: The brand, after all, was a go-to in the aughts for young women who needed thigh-high boots and beige platform pumps but wouldn\xe2\x80\x99t pay Louboutin prices. (Obviously, you\xe2\x80\x99d wear them with Theory blazers and Calypso dresses.) Weitzman , the founder, sold his concern to Tapestry when he retired because he wanted it to be operated by a real owner. One could argue that Stuart Weitzman\xe2\x80\x99s time has passed, and it should simply be phased out to make room for the Larroud\xc3\xa9s of the world, but at least Caleres is in the business of selling shoes. (Neither Tapestry nor Caleres responded to requests for comment.)

    The next question is what Tapestry does with Kate Spade New York, a truly missed opportunity given the previous effectiveness of the brand\xe2\x80\x99s life-events-driven selling strategy (wear a KSNY dress to the prom; get their china on your registry). Also, let\xe2\x80\x99s see whether Tapestry C.E.O. Joanne Crevoiserat once again becomes acquisitive now that the Capri mess is behind her. \xe2\x80\x94 Lauren Sherman

And now, the main event\xe2\x80\xa6

The Week in Shopping: White Lotus Fever & The Row\xe2\x80\x99s Handbag Trick

The return of HBO\xe2\x80\x99s wealth-porn dark comedy franchise has spawned some successful collabs (everyone\xe2\x80\x99s talking about Parker Posey\xe2\x80\x99s outfits) and some hotel gift shop kitsch. Meanwhile, that Margaux bag hasn\xe2\x80\x99t disappeared.

Sarah Shapiro

The luxury world had a collective meltdown this week over rumors that The Row\xe2\x80\x99s status-symbol Margaux handbag would be discontinued. First thing\xe2\x80\x99s first: It\xe2\x80\x99s not, calm down. No need to panic buy or search for \xe2\x80\x9cthe next Margaux.\xe2\x80\x9d (For the inspired, Lyst tells me they\xe2\x80\x99ve seen a 262 percent increase in demand for Cos\xe2\x80\x99s bowling bag in the past three months.)

Instead, The Row is wisely tightening the bag\xe2\x80\x99s distribution to create more scarcity, boost exclusivity (of an already hard-to-get item), and maintain iron-clad control over who sells it. Going forward, you might need a direct hookup with a sales associate from The Row\xe2\x80\x99s own retail stores instead of simply heading to Net-a-Porter or Mytheresa ; the third-party retailer inventory is indeed drying up. The strategy will frustrate some retailers, and customers who don\xe2\x80\x99t regularly visit The Row on Melrose Place or the Upper East Side, or in Paris or London, but this kind of inventory management and control maintenance is pretty standard in luxury fashion\xe2\x80\x94especially when people are buying up stock just to resell for a profit.

Relatedly, there was some good news for Net-a-Porter this week: the launch of the Ralph Lauren \xe2\x80\x9cPolo Country at Net\xe2\x80\x9d capsule collection. (A mouthful, yes.) While Ralph Lauren can feel very Choate (tartan kilts, thick stripe rugby polos, cable knit cardigans ), Net x Ralph Lauren is leaning into the territory of Ralph\xe2\x80\x99s Colorado ranch. This is a big win for Net, as rancher fashion is hot right now\xe2\x80\x94perhaps a function of Beyonc\xc3\xa9 \xe2\x80\x99s Cowboy Carter influence as well as Yellowstone and Landman , which are popularizing cowboy boots , work jackets , and concho belts . Ralph Lauren, meanwhile, is one of the few desirable American brands at the moment, with a history of leveraging patriotism-as-style. Done right, this sort of capsule collection offers a tight enough assortment to get the idea across in a few key pieces you can\xe2\x80\x99t get elsewhere. Net-a-Porter\xe2\x80\x99s global audience doesn\xe2\x80\x99t need Ralph\xe2\x80\x99s elegant appropriations of English and French aristocracy, they need cowboys.

The Collab Report

Miu Miu continues to influence every mall brand\xe2\x80\x99s lineup, from striped tees and shirting to the whole boxers-peeking-out-from-the-top-of-pants thing that I wrote about last week. Now, the queen of the trickle-down is actually trickling down with the launch of Miu Miu x Petit Bateau , a little line of tees and other underpinnings that are all under $1,000. (Not nothing, but most luxury goods are really something these days.)

The capsule gives off \xe2\x80\x9990s middle schooler vibes, with a bandeau layered over a t-shirt and a bodysuit reminiscent of a newborn onesie. (Not a drag, just a fact.) It\xe2\x80\x99s nostalgic, and will allow young fashion obsessives using all the Miu Miu styling tricks to incorporate some actual Miu Miu into their wardrobes. Petit Bateau already has plenty of legitimacy among consumers who like dressing as if they\xe2\x80\x99re wearing a school uniform.

But back to the diffusion of Miu Miu\xe2\x80\x99s perfectly boxy, multicolor-striped t-shirts and polo shirts that are everywhere right now (see: Gap , Reformation , Free People , Zara , Abercrombie , and Lioness at Revolve and Shopbop ), first seen on the Spring/Summer 2024 runway. By last summer, top-top influencer Courtney Grow was wearing versions from Uniqlo and Kotn . While you currently can\xe2\x80\x99t escape the skater tees, I predict this will soon evolve into a return to the classic French Breton stripe. (Miu Miu is already selling boatneck sweaters.) It\xe2\x80\x99s all of a piece.

But the Collab Strategy of the Week award goes to HBO\xe2\x80\x99s The White Lotu s, which seemed to partner with absolutely everyone\xe2\x80\x94fair enough, since the show\xe2\x80\x99s Season 3 premiere drew 2.4 million viewers, more than double its previous season debut. Some partnerships make sense. Away x The White Lotus is thoughtful in its materials, colors, and marketing\xe2\x80\x94they hired Lezio Lopes , the illustrator who worked on the show\xe2\x80\x94and it\xe2\x80\x99s the perfect timing for vacation gear. Other stuff looks like what you would find in a poorly merchandised resort \xe2\x80\x9cboutique.\xe2\x80\x9d CB2 \xe2\x80\x99s take is hotel-room-inspired; Bloomingdale\xe2\x80\x99s private label Aqua released a line mimicking the Lilly-Pulitzer-goes-abroad feel of the cast\xe2\x80\x99s costumes; Abercrombie & Fitch is selling merchy t-shirts, hats, and sweatshirts that belong in a hotel gift shop; and H&M \xe2\x80\x99s homage feels Zimmerman-esque. (Apropos, given that women who frequent resorts like those in The White Lotus love Zimmerman.)

There was a lot of online chatter regarding a Banana Republic outfit worn by Parker Posey \xe2\x80\x99s character. Banana also dressed Patrick Schwarzenegger , who plays her son, in a custom suit for the premiere. (He\xe2\x80\x99s hosting an event in New York in early March to launch the TWL x BR collaboration.) Then there were all the Gucci synergies, as Lauren noted earlier this week. Also, Tombolo did the hotel staff uniforms. The integrations are truly endless in a Barbie movie sort of way. Remember that Gap Inc. C.E.O. Richard Dickson , who was a Mattel exec at the time of the Barbie frenzy, takes credit for its dozens upon dozens of partnerships.

Clearly, there\xe2\x80\x99s a good deal of irony in the fact that this particular show, which skewers luxury consumption and excess, is participating so enthusiastically in creating more merchandise. On the other hand, whatever it skewers\xe2\x80\xa6 it\xe2\x80\x99s a business.

The Week in Feedback\xe2\x80\xa6

On the end of the downtown Dallas Neiman Marcus : \xe2\x80\x9cAs a Dallas native who still subscribes to The Dallas Morning News , I am catatonic over the store closure. TBH, the store wasn\xe2\x80\x99t that great. Didn\xe2\x80\x99t carry all the designers. And frankly, no one in Dallas likes going to downtown downtown. It has no charm. But doing lunch during Christmastime at the Zodiac Room was something many of us grew up with. And all of that going away just hurts.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A strategy guy

On Neiman\xe2\x80\x99s Dallas, but also on Catherine Bloom departing for Nordstrom : \xe2\x80\x9cWhen I read \xe2\x80\x98A Neiman Marcus Tragedy,\xe2\x80\x99 I thought you were referring to their loss of Catherine Bloom to Nordstrom. While the closing of the NM downtown store is sad, the reality is that it had not been profitable for years. Dallas ladies simply will not go downtown, no matter how much money Tim Headington spends trying to revitalize it, especially when Highland Park Village exists. ... Meanwhile, Catherine was a powerhouse revenue generator for NM Beverly. Her defection is tragic for NM in my book.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A former retail exec

Yes, more on Neiman\xe2\x80\x99s Dallas : \xe2\x80\x9cFor those mourning the loss of the Dallas Neiman Marcus, it was the subject of a documentary by Frederick Wiseman , called simply The Store . It includes lines such as, \xe2\x80\x98Remember, every day here, you\xe2\x80\x99re selling. Everything you do, every interaction you have, every breath you take, needs to be in the service of selling. But it shouldn\xe2\x80\x99t look like that. We\xe2\x80\x99re not that kind of store. Our customers don\xe2\x80\x99t appreciate being sold to.\xe2\x80\x99 It screens tonight at Lincoln Center as part of a Wiseman retrospective. Would love to see someone film the tail end of the store\xe2\x80\x99s life.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A documentary filmmaker

On the price-value equation : \xe2\x80\x9cAs someone who buys a lot of The Row and many other brands (now and historically), I\xe2\x80\x99m confused by the relentless narrative that The Row is so expensive. Yes, it\xe2\x80\x99s certainly not affordable to most, but it\xe2\x80\x99s comparable to Louis Vuitton, Prada, Gucci, YSL, and yet better quality than all those brands. It\xe2\x80\x99s less expensive than Bottega, Loro Piana, Chanel, and Herm\xc3\xa8s, but just as well made, if not better. I wonder if some people just like to shit on The Row because it\xe2\x80\x99s not a heritage European house but a \xe2\x80\x98celebrity\xe2\x80\x99 brand.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A designer

Have a great weekend,
Lauren

P.S. : We are using affiliate links because we are a business. We may make a couple bucks off them.

Puck fashion correspondent Lauren Sherman and a rotating cast of industry insiders take you deep behind the scenes of this multitrillion-dollar biz, from creative director switcheroos to M&A drama, D.T.C. downfalls, and magazine mishaps. Fashion People is an extension of Line Sheet, Lauren\xe2\x80\x99s private email for Puck, where she tracks what\xe2\x80\x99s happening beyond the press releases in fashion, beauty, and media. New episodes publish every Tuesday and Friday.

Puck\xe2\x80\x99s daily art market email, anchored by industry expert Marion Maneker, offers unparalleled access to the mega-auctions and galleries, elite buyers and sellers, and the power players who run this opaque world. Wall Power also features Julie Brener Davich, a veteran of Christie\xe2\x80\x99s and Sotheby\xe2\x80\x99s, who provides unique insights into how the business really works.

Amazon\xe2\x80\x99s James Bond Coup

MATTHEW BELLONI

NASCAR\xe2\x80\x99s Loyalty Test

JOHN OURAND

Rubio\xe2\x80\x99s Shadow Secretary

TARA PALMERI
